summary:
Trichloroacetic acid (TCA) was identified as the most effective local treatment due to its ability to precisely seal dentinal tubules, providing reliable relief against molar hypersensitivity compared to other options like alcohol, chlorhexidine, or silver nitrate which lack similar efficacy while potentially causing more discomfort or reduced performance. Key factors include TCA’s targeted action minimizing side effects ensuring efficient management of severe sensitivity issues typically found in molars where comprehensive care must balance rapid symptom reduction alongside patient tolerance considerations essential when selecting a long-term solution tailored specifically towards these complex cases involving extensive tooth surface exposure
